reasoning: The knowledge cutoff date is October 26, 2025, leaving just over two months (about 66 days) until the December 31, 2025 resolution date. For the 25th Amendment (Section 4) to be invoked and successfully lead to the President's removal, it would require the Vice President and a majority of the Cabinet to declare the President unable to discharge his duties. Furthermore, if the President contests this declaration, it would necessitate a two-thirds supermajority vote in BOTH chambers of Congress to sustain the removal. Given the extreme partisan polarization in Congress, Trump's active political status as of late October 2025, and the loyalty of his Cabinet and Vice President JD Vance, the political viability of achieving a two-thirds majority in both the House and the Senate within a two-month timeframe is near zero. While Polymarket odds briefly spiked to ~7% in early September due to unfounded rumors, they fundamentally reflect long-tail speculation rather than a realistic constitutional pathway. Considering the exceedingly high procedural hurdles and the very short remaining timeline, the probability of this outcome is near the minimum possible bound.
